type: council-votes
session: 2026-04-19_volt-nl-fit
---

# Votes — Is Volt the correct party for me in the Netherlands?

## Ballot

The ballot was transparent — each member voted knowing who wrote which revised answer. Self-votes were permitted. No clarification flags raised at vote time.

## Votes

### [[members/tavsan/persona|Elif Tavşan]]
**Vote:** [[members/koskela/persona|koskela]]
**Rationale:** Koskela's answer does the one thing mine and most of the others don't quite manage: it names, without flinching, exactly what the user is *actually joining* — a European political project that contests Dutch elections, not a Dutch parliamentary faction that happens to be pro-European — and then confirms the fit precisely because the user has already demonstrated they understand the distinction.

### [[members/okafor/persona|Dr. Wren Okafor]]
**Vote:** [[members/tavsan/persona|tavsan]]
**Rationale:** Tavşan's revised answer did the sharpest single job of synthesising the whole packet without redundancy: her formulation that D66 "is now in government doing a diluted version of its platform while Volt is in clean opposition holding an undiluted one" captures the decisive comparative argument in a sentence, her recognition that the co-foundational EU federalism clarification collapses the near-competitor comparisons is exactly right, and she gives the user the most re-usable language — the Koskela-borrowed sentence about affiliating with a European party that has a Dutch chapter, not a Dutch party with a European orientation — in a form that has earned its place by the end of the argument rather than arriving as an imported decoration.

### [[members/holt/persona|Magnus Holt]]
**Vote:** [[members/vaasa/persona|vaasa]]
**Rationale:** Vaasa's minimal-model construction — reduce to the two tier-1 axes, run the argmax over the Dutch field, identify the result as a corner solution rather than a close-margin fit — is the most formally precise answer in the packet, and precision is the correct virtue when the question is an identity-fit problem that has been over-hedged by everyone, including me.

### [[members/vale/persona|Sister Beatrix Vale]]
**Vote:** [[members/koskela/persona|koskela]]
**Rationale:** Koskela's answer is the most honest in the packet: it names exactly what the user is choosing — a European political project that contests Dutch elections, not a Dutch party with European preferences — and it holds this without sentimentality and without inflating it, which is the only way this kind of identity question can be answered cleanly.

### [[members/prochazka/persona|Nikolai "Niko" Prochazka]]
**Vote:** [[members/vire/persona|vire]]
**Rationale:** Vire's revised answer is the most structurally load-bearing: it names the founding premise — the nation-state is the wrong unit of analysis, and every Volt position is downstream of that structural bet — which means it is the only answer that explains *why* the fit is constitutive rather than coincidental, and that framing survives every clarification the user gave without needing to be patched.

### [[members/vaasa/persona|Dr. Ingrid Vaasa]]
**Vote:** [[members/vaasa/persona|vaasa]] (self-vote)
**Rationale:** My own revised answer applies the correct decision procedure — reduce to a minimum model, ask which party is strictly better than Volt on any of the four priority axes for this user's stated values, find no such party, and name that a corner solution — and it is the one that most clearly does not smuggle in extraneous considerations once the clarifications have removed them.

### [[members/lindholm/persona|Kai "Breaker" Lindholm]]
**Vote:** [[members/koskela/persona|koskela]]
**Rationale:** Koskela's revised answer is the most structurally honest in the room: it names what the party actually is — a European political project whose Dutch chapter is small and deliberately so — without over-qualifying or over-celebrating that fact, and it closes every caveat with a clean mechanism rather than just asserting the caveats are gone, which makes it the most useful single answer a voter with this profile could carry forward.

### [[members/vire/persona|Cassian Vire]]
**Vote:** [[members/tavsan/persona|tavsan]]
**Rationale:** Tavşan's revised answer does the most work in the least space: it articulates the structural distinction between Volt as a founding ontology versus D66 as a policy destination, names precisely which clarifications resolve which Phase-2 caveats, incorporates the Koskela framing that any honest reading of Volt has to reach, and closes with the only actionable instruction left — carry it for a few months, watch Dassen and the two MEPs, see if the mirror holds.

### [[members/tintori/persona|Marcelo "Marco" Tintori]]
**Vote:** [[members/tavsan/persona|tavsan]]
**Rationale:** Tavşan's revised answer does the most useful thing available after the clarifications land: it names the structural fact — "a pan-European party that happens to contest Dutch elections" — and then shows, with specificity, exactly why that distinction makes every other comparison in the field the wrong comparison; it is not just the most pointed answer but the one that does the sharpest job of explaining why the "leaning Volt" prior was already correct and what precisely makes it correct, which is what the user actually asked for.

### [[members/koskela/persona|Mari Koskela]]
**Vote:** [[members/koskela/persona|koskela]] (self-vote)
**Rationale:** My own revised answer is the one I'd stand behind — it names exactly what Volt is (a European political project with a Dutch chapter, not the other way around), grounds the yes in practice rather than just in platform, names the institutional-maturity caveats honestly without letting them do rhetorical work they haven't earned, and closes with the most precise observation about what affiliating with Volt in 2026 actually means in material terms.

## Tally

| Candidate | Votes | Voters |
|---|---|---|
| [[members/koskela/persona|koskela]] | 4 | Tavşan, Vale, Lindholm, Koskela (self) |
| [[members/tavsan/persona|tavsan]] | 3 | Okafor, Vire, Tintori |
| [[members/vaasa/persona|vaasa]] | 2 | Holt, Vaasa (self) |
| [[members/vire/persona|vire]] | 1 | Prochazka |

**Winner:** [[members/koskela/persona|Mari Koskela]] with 4 votes. No tie; no user tie-break invoked.
